assertion: Analysis 'model-collapse-mechanism-theory-confirmation'. Supporting: Shows generative models degrade when trained on their own output — model collapse: the tails of the distribution vanish first. Peer-reviewed as 'AI models collapse when trained on recursively generated data', Nature 631 (2024); open access on arXiv. Analyzes autophagous training loops across image models: without enough fresh real data in each generation, quality or diversity progressively decays — termed Model Autophagy Disorder (MAD). Open access on arXiv; no further formal venue recorded in the atlas. Shows collapse is not fate: accumulating synthetic data alongside real data — instead of replacing it — bounds the error across LLMs, diffusion models and VAEs. Also published at COLM 2024; open access on arXiv.

conditions: work must be catalogued in the pinned works-atlas snapshot, not merely mentioned in secondary commentary
theory-side corroboration is drawn only from the pinned theory-atlas snapshot's own model-collapse keyword match over tags/summary/work
